rationale: The cited 2009 JMR paper directly studies the same core context effects (compromise and attraction) and links them to deliberative trade-off processing via resource depletion, which closely overlaps the 2011 paper’s mechanism-focused argument about reduced compensatory trade-offs under high construal. Given this strong construct and effect-level alignment in the notes, this citation is plausibly a substantive theoretical/mechanism reference rather than an incidental mention. The DOI-level match confirms it is an intentional bibliographic citation.
